scopeNote: Re-ran the configured milestone validators after the case-insensitive duplicate-account fix and GST report-owner convergence fix, then re-reviewed only the two fix features tied to the round-1 blocking findings. The scoped test suite, compile/typecheck, and Spotless lint check all passed. The duplicate-account fix passed scrutiny, but the GST convergence fix still regresses the public GST report payload by dropping rate-bucket/detail output, so the milestone remains blocked from user testing.

blockingIssues.description: The fix removes the legacy report-side GST math owner, but `/api/v1/reports/gst-return` now hardcodes empty `rateSummaries` and `transactionDetails` in `ReportService.gstReturn()`, regressing the public GST report contract that still requires rate-bucket output and usable detail data. See `.factory/validation/journal-and-account-core/scrutiny/reviews/gst-report-owner-convergence-hardcut.json`.

appliedUpdates.description: Archived the prior round-1 scrutiny synthesis before recording the rerun outcome.
Recorded the round-2 scrutiny synthesis for the journal-and-account-core rerun, including rerun validator results and fix-feature review outcomes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the accounting module guidance to name `AccountResolutionOwnerService` as the controller-facing canonical owner for chart-of-accounts and default-account API resolution instead of implying `AccountingService` alone owns that corridor.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The round-2 passing review for `account-code-case-insensitive-uniqueness-hardcut` again found `AccountController` routing account/default-account/tree actions through `AccountResolutionOwnerService` while the module guidance still points workers only at `AccountingService`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that narrowly scoped runtime/API fixes can satisfy proof requirements with deterministic integration regressions when those tests fully exercise the public contract and concurrency/persistence guarantee, instead of always requiring compose-backed curl proof.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The round-2 review for `account-code-case-insensitive-uniqueness-hardcut` accepted the fix based on serial API duplicate rejection plus concurrent DB-level fail-closed regression coverage and flagged the current skill wording as too rigid for that fix class.

rejectedObservations.observation: The GST report validation contract/test mismatch around `rateSummaries` and `transactionDetails` coverage.
rejectedObservations.reason: Rejected as a shared-state update candidate because the defect is already captured as the current blocking feature issue in `gst-report-owner-convergence-hardcut` and does not yet justify a separate services/library/AGENTS/skill update.
